options.text: degrowth — deliberately shrinking the economy
slow economic growth
a drop in temperatures

exercises.explanation: Not slower growth: the deliberate choice to produce and consume less. The word that splits every debate.

exercises.prompt: The C1 response to a provocative text is to…

options.text: concede what's defensible, refuse what isn't, stay calm
match its outrage with your own
ignore its arguments entirely

exercises.explanation: Composure: « le doute fait partie de la science — pas du déni ».

exercises.template: Le sommet s'est conclu sur des {{b1}} non contraignants.
exercises.explanation: Des engagements — commitments; non contraignants = nobody is bound (treaty-unit echo).

exercises.prompt: The provocateur's favourite move against consensus.
exercises.template: Son article prend la pensée dominante à {{b1}}.
exercises.explanation: Prendre à contre-pied — to wrong-foot. À contre-jour is backlighting; à contrecœur is reluctantly.

exercises.prompt: « La culpabilité écologique se vend très bien » is provocative because it targets…

options.text: the reader's own good conscience — your guilt as a marketing product
the price of solar panels
a specific politician

exercises.explanation: The sharpest provocations aim at the audience, not the opponent.

exercises.prompt: What a project without numbers is (one word).
exercises.template: Un projet sans chiffres est un {{b1}}.
exercises.explanation: Chiffrer — defending a project means giving numbers, or it's poetry.

exercises.prompt: Match each energy word to its English meaning.

pairs.left: les gaz à effet de serre

exercises.prompt: Put the words in order: "Renewables cover a third of the needs."
Listen to the project defense. Which moves does the speaker chain together?
exercises.transcript: On me dira que ce quartier sans voitures coûte cher — mais trois degrés de plus coûteront davantage que tous nos programmes de sobriété réunis.

options.text: Anticipating the objection, then answering it with a number
Insulting the opponents, then leaving
Listing sponsors, then thanking the mayor

exercises.explanation: « On me dira que… mais » + chiffrer: the two load-bearing moves of défendre un projet.

exercises.prompt: Read this provocative column and answer both questions.
exercises.passageFr: Osons le dire : le tri des déchets est le rosaire des temps modernes. Chaque pot de yaourt rincé achète une indulgence ; l'empreinte carbone du vol Paris-Bali, elle, est pardonnée d'avance. La culpabilité écologique est devenue une économie florissante — on vend l'absolution en vrac, et la pensée dominante applaudit. Que les chiffres soient têtus, c'est entendu : 80 % de l'empreinte d'un ménage ne passe ni par sa poubelle ni par son bac jaune. Mais qui veut d'un chiffre quand on peut avoir une bonne conscience ?
exercises.passageEn: Let's dare to say it: sorting your rubbish is the rosary of modern times. Every rinsed yoghurt pot buys an indulgence; the carbon footprint of the Paris-Bali flight is forgiven in advance. Eco-guilt has become a flourishing economy — absolution is sold in bulk, and the dominant thinking applauds. That the figures are stubborn is well known: 80% of a household's footprint goes through neither its bin nor its recycling box. But who wants a figure when you can have a clear conscience?

questions.prompt: What is the column's provocation strategy?

options.text: Religious metaphors (rosary, indulgences) that mock the reader's own rituals
A neutral list of recycling statistics
A personal attack on one named minister

questions.prompt: What defensible point hides under the provocation?

options.text: Most of a household's footprint isn't addressed by sorting waste
Recycling is illegal in France
Flights to Bali have no emissions

exercises.prompt: « Le doute fait partie de la science — pas du déni » concedes and refuses what?

options.text: Concedes that doubt is scientifically healthy; refuses doubt as a cover for denial
Concedes nothing; refuses everything
Concedes that science is finished; refuses new research

exercises.explanation: The concede-refuse pattern: give the provocateur his defensible inch, not the mile.

exercises.prompt: Listen, then choose what it means.
exercises.transcript: Le sommet s'est conclu sur des engagements non contraignants.

options.text: The summit ended with non-binding commitments.
Degrowth? A religion for the well-fed, the provocateur throws out.
Concede the doubt, refuse the denial, give a number.

exercises.prompt: What does « le réchauffement climatique » mean?

options.text: global warming
greenhouse gases
carbon footprint

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word (from this unit's vocabulary).
exercises.explanation: « La décroissance divise jusqu'aux écologistes. » — degrowth.

exercises.prompt: Match each word to its English meaning.
Put the words in order: “A project without numbers is a poem.”
Speaking practice — listen, then say the sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Le sommet s'est conclu sur des engagements non contraignants.
exercises.targetEn: The summit ended with non-binding commitments.

exercises.prompt: Speaking practice — say this sentence aloud and record yourself:
exercises.targetFr: Concédez le doute, refusez le déni, donnez un chiffre.
exercises.targetEn: Concede the doubt, refuse the denial, give a number.
